agency: NYC Department of Health and Mental Hygiene (DOHMH)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting; www.nyc.gov robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Crawled the DOH section of the shared NYC.gov 'LiveSite' WCM (/site/doh/*.page) — homepage, services (birth-death-records, restaurant-grades, allclinics, sexual-health-clinics), business/permits-licenses. Fingerprinted the transactional back-office apps on a816-*.nyc.gov (ABC Eats, eVital VRRTS, Accela Citizen Access, NYC Health Map) from response headers + page markup. Open Data verified and enumerated via the Socrata Discovery API filtered on Dataset-Information_Agency = 'Department of Health and Mental Hygiene (DOHMH)' (81 assets).
headline_findings: DOHMH is the most DATA-RICH domain in the project: 81 datasets on NYC Open Data, led by 'DOHMH New York City Restaurant Inspection Results' (43nn-pn8j, ~406k views) — among the single most-viewed datasets in all of NYC Open Data.

But its TRANSACTIONAL services are siloed in separate legacy apps with no open, agent-native API: ABC Eats (restaurant grade lookup; AngularJS + ASP.NET MVC), eVital VRRTS + VitalChek (birth/death certificate orders), and Accela Citizen Access (health permits & licenses).

The public restaurant grade lookup (ABC Eats) is a JavaScript SPA over a private Web API — the same famous data that is ALSO fully open on Socrata, so the read need is met twice while the write/transaction need is met zero times.
The one true net-new WRITE surface is ordering a vital record (birth/death certificate): a paid transaction locked inside eVital/VitalChek with no open API.
Reframe: Parks=replatform, DOE=reclaim, Council=consolidate+own, DOHMH=TRANSACT — the open data is solved; the gap is agent-native, transactional services. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.type: private Web API behind an AngularJS SPA
apis_observed.note: Public restaurant grade/inspection lookup; the SPA fetches JSON from an internal, undocumented backend API — not an open contract.

apis_observed.owner: DOHMH Office of Vital Records (ASP.NET MVC 5.2 / IIS 10)
apis_observed.note: eVital VRRTS — birth/death certificate request tracking; no API surface.
Accela Citizen Access — health permits & licenses; vendor product, no open API exposed.
Clinic & service-site locator; likely ArcGIS/service backend, no documented API.
81 DOHMH datasets incl. the famous restaurant inspections (43nn-pn8j) — fully open, machine-readable SODA endpoints.
Complaint intake routed to DOHMH (indoor environmental complaints surface on Open Data 9jgj-bmct).
Third-party online birth/death certificate ordering + payment.
Real-user monitoring injected across NYC.gov + a816 apps.

fruit.name: Restaurant / Food Establishment grades
opendata_match: DOHMH New York City Restaurant Inspection Results 43nn-pn8j (31c)
fruit.notes: The city's flagship food-safety data. Public ABC Eats lookup is a JS SPA over a private API; the same data is fully open on Socrata.

fruit.name: Restaurant inspections & violations
opendata_match: DOHMH New York City Restaurant Inspection Results 43nn-pn8j (31c)
fruit.notes: One row per violation; scores/grades/critical flags. Modeled as an Inspection with a rolled-up violations array.

fruit.name: Rodent (rat) inspections
fruit.notes: Rat Mitigation program inspections keyed to tax lot (BBL); result flags active rat activity.

fruit.name: Childcare center inspections
fruit.notes: Permitted daycares with capacity, program type, and violation-rate outcomes.

fruit.name: Health clinics & service sites
fruit.notes: Sexual health, immunization, TB, mental-health clinics via NYC Health Map. No open dataset — reverse-engineered as a structured resource.

fruit.name: Indoor environmental / health complaints
fruit.notes: Mold, indoor air, pests, lead — routed from 311; intake itself has no open API.

fruit.name: Health permits & licenses
fruit.notes: Food-service and other DOHMH permits via Accela Citizen Access (vendor COTS). No open API; an alternate net-new write candidate.

fruit.name: Order a birth / death certificate
fruit.notes: The one true net-new WRITE surface: a paid vital-records order locked inside eVital VRRTS and the VitalChek vendor portal, with no open, agent-native API.

fruit.name: NYC Community Health Profiles - Public Use Dataset (2026)
fruit.notes: Neighborhood-level health indicators (59 community districts).

fruit.name: NYC Community Health Survey - Variable Crosswalk
fruit.notes: Year-by-year variable crosswalk for the Community Health Survey.

fruit.name: NYC Intercensal Population Estimates 2020
fruit.notes: EpiSurveillance denominator population table (2020 census base).

fruit.name: NYC Intercensal Population Estimates 2010
fruit.notes: 2010-base population denominators.

fruit.name: NYC Intercensal Population Estimates 1990-2000
fruit.notes: Historical population denominator series.

fruit.name: HIV Surveillance Mid-Year Report 2025 - Data Tables
fruit.notes: Data tables underlying the HIV/AIDS mid-year surveillance report.

fruit.notes: Public-use birth dataset by community district (annual series 2000-2021).

fruit.notes: Public-use birth counts by census tract, rolling 5-year windows.

fruit.notes: Public-use birth counts by ZIP, rolling windows.
